tagline: The miller's daughter who came home with a forager's eye.
leadParagraph: Wren returns to Hollowfen after three winters away and finds the mill silent, the village hungry, and her father's hidden journal waiting in a drawer.
backgroundParagraph: Born above Tobin's mill on the River Wend, Wren grew up among grain dust, ledgers, and the quiet habits of a father who knew more about mushrooms than he ever admitted aloud.
perspectiveParagraph: Wren reads habitat the way other people read faces. Damp bark, shadow, season, and soil tell her what will fruit next and whether the woods are safe to trust.
pullquote: I thought coming home would feel like stepping backward. It feels more like opening a door no one has touched in years.

missions.title: Homecoming
missions.summary: Return to Hollowfen and reach the village square.

missions.title: The Crooked Pintle
missions.summary: Meet Bram and recover the key to the mill.

missions.title: Your Father's Mill
missions.summary: Enter her childhood home and search the silent mill.

missions.title: The Hidden Journal
missions.summary: Find Tobin's hidden mushroom journal.

missions.title: The First Forage
missions.summary: Gather the safe basics and a gold-stemmed find at the Old Wood edge.

missions.title: Marra's Kitchen
missions.summary: Bring the first basket to the Crooked Pintle.

missions.title: A Knock at the Door
missions.summary: Hear what Sister Almy knows about Wren's family.

missions.title: The Vine-Tender's Lessons
missions.summary: Learn cultivation and plant Wood Ear at the mill.

missions.title: Joren's Forge
missions.summary: Commission a proper foraging knife from Joren.

missions.title: Twelve Silver by Yule
missions.summary: Raise twelve silver before the Wenmar cottage is seized.

missions.title: The Trader's Ledger
missions.summary: Open a regular trade route with Theo.

missions.title: Brightspore at the Bedside
missions.summary: Find Brightspore and carry Marra's tonic to Edda's grandfather.

missions.title: A Stranger at the Inn
missions.summary: Meet the quiet traveler who asked for Wren by name.

missions.title: Two Boards Come Down
missions.summary: Fund repairs and see two cottages reopen.

missions.title: Father Calden's Doubt
missions.summary: Hear Calden's warning and visit the chapel garden gate.

missions.title: Hollin's Inheritance
missions.summary: Compare the page Hollin carries with Tobin's journal.

missions.title: The Witch's Cottage
missions.summary: Follow the old path into the Deep Wood.

missions.title: The Wend's True Course
missions.summary: Use Wendlight to trace the river's old course.

missions.title: The Chapel Garden Opens
missions.summary: Bring the Wendlight truth to Father Calden.

missions.title: Edda Asks
missions.summary: Hear Edda's carefully practiced request.

missions.title: Theo's Capital
missions.summary: Listen to Theo's offer and weigh what it would cost.

missions.title: The First Festival in Three Years
missions.summary: Gather four ingredients for Marra's festival dishes.

missions.title: A Sealed Letter
missions.summary: Receive the letter Voss was ordered to deliver in person.

missions.title: The Lord's Offer
missions.summary: Read Lord Aldric's sealed offer at the mill.

missions.title: The Source of the Wend
missions.summary: Follow the old river line upstream.

missions.title: The Meeting
missions.summary: Take the evidence to Aldric's manor and answer his offer.

quotes.text: Near trees.
quotes.context: Dryly answering Theo's question about Goldfoot.

quotes.text: I concede arithmetic.
quotes.context: Meeting Lord Aldric at the manor.

quotes.text: The question is what you think prosperous is allowed to cost.

keepsake: The mill key wrapped in a rag
tagline: The old innkeeper who kept a key and a place at the hearth.
leadParagraph: Bram is the first familiar face Wren sees on returning to Hollowfen: broad, white-bearded, and careful with every word he has waited years to say.
backgroundParagraph: He keeps the Crooked Pintle open through lean seasons, buys Wren's first basket, and remembers both the Tobin family and the village before the Wend changed course.
perspectiveParagraph: Bram measures recovery in warm bowls, occupied chairs, familiar lantern hooks, and whether the inn can still make room for someone coming home.
pullquote: Lord help me. You've got your father's height.

missions.title: Homecoming
missions.summary: Recognize Wren at the village well.

missions.title: The Crooked Pintle
missions.summary: Give Wren the mill key and what news he can bear to tell.

missions.title: Marra's Kitchen
missions.summary: Buy Wren's first basket and bring Marra into the room.

missions.title: A Stranger at the Inn
missions.summary: Host the traveler who asks for Wren by name.

missions.title: The First Festival in Three Years
missions.summary: Restore the Pintle's lanterns and public place in village life.

quotes.text: Lord help me. You've got your father's height.
Wheel hasn't turned since the Wend went and changed its mind about where it was going.
quotes.context: Explaining what happened to the mill.

quotes.text: I remember where the lantern hooks go. Mostly.

tagline: The cook who can make a meal, a lesson, and a warning from one basket.
leadParagraph: Marra is Hollowfen's compact force of kitchen authority, with rolled sleeves, strong arms, and no patience for careless handling of good ingredients.
backgroundParagraph: She turns Wren's first forage into a meal that brings people back to the Crooked Pintle, then becomes the village's recipe mentor and most exacting buyer.
perspectiveParagraph: Marra trusts texture, temperature, and useful work. Gratitude comes out as instruction, and hope is something she prefers to serve in a bowl.
pullquote: I'll tell you twice and not three times.

missions.title: Marra's Kitchen
missions.summary: Inspect Wren's first basket and turn it into a proper meal.

missions.title: Brightspore at the Bedside
missions.summary: Prepare the bitter tonic for Edda's grandfather.

missions.title: Two Boards Come Down
missions.summary: Prepare for returning households that will need bread.

missions.title: The First Festival in Three Years
missions.summary: Name and cook the four festival dishes.

quotes.text: You wash these in cold water. Not hot. Hot bruises them and makes liars of the texture.
quotes.context: Teaching Wren how to handle the first basket.

quotes.text: I'll tell you twice and not three times.
quotes.context: Establishing the rules of her kitchen.

quotes.text: Four dishes by sundown. If anyone calls it rustic, I will make them eat the garnish.

tagline: The watchful girl at the path's edge who wants to learn properly.
leadParagraph: Edda is small, serious, and intensely observant, watching Wren's work from the edge until she is ready to ask for a place in it.
backgroundParagraph: She carries a small basket, cares closely for her grandfather, and treats mushroom knowledge as something practical enough to save a household.
perspectiveParagraph: Edda listens before speaking and tests every lesson against what it can do. She is less interested in grand words than in names, false names, and where to cut.
pullquote: Do not make me say the obvious part. I practiced the other part.

missions.title: The First Forage
missions.summary: Watch Wren enter the Edge Woods and gather safely.

missions.title: Brightspore at the Bedside
missions.summary: Ask Wren to help a grandfather who has stopped eating.

missions.title: Edda Asks
missions.summary: Ask to become Wren's formal apprentice.

missions.title: The First Festival in Three Years
missions.summary: Help at the square and notice when Wren is hiding with dishes.

quotes.text: Grandfather hasn't eaten since Monday.
That is not a big enough word.
quotes.context: Answering Wren after her grandfather eats a full bowl.

quotes.text: Do not make me say the obvious part. I practiced the other part.

home: Almy's garden and the chapel garden
tagline: The severe teacher whose hands remember what the village tried to forget.
leadParagraph: Sister Almy is slight, wiry, and direct, a vine-tender whose weathered hands make every cultivation lesson look precise and necessary.
backgroundParagraph: She recognizes knowledge in Wren that the younger woman did not know she carried and begins teaching her how to read rot, feed it, and leave enough behind.
perspectiveParagraph: Almy treats patience as a discipline and names as responsibilities. Most useful lessons sound like warnings when she gives them.
pullquote: Do not rush growth. Rushed mushrooms teach regret.

missions.title: A Knock at the Door
missions.summary: Tell Wren that her family's knowledge reaches back another generation.

missions.title: The Vine-Tender's Lessons
missions.summary: Teach Wren the foundations of mushroom cultivation.

missions.title: Brightspore at the Bedside
missions.summary: Explain the medicinal use and careful harvest of Brightspore.

missions.title: Hollin's Inheritance
missions.summary: Recognize the name and page Hollin carries.

missions.title: The Witch's Cottage
missions.summary: Walk the old path with Wren and Hollin.

missions.title: The Chapel Garden Opens
missions.summary: See the chapel garden returned to useful work.

quotes.text: No. But your hands do.
quotes.context: Answering Wren's claim that she does not remember her grandmother.

quotes.text: Most useful lessons are.
quotes.context: On lessons that sound like warnings.

quotes.text: Do not rush growth. Rushed mushrooms teach regret.

tagline: The smith who respects a clean edge, honest payment, and useful work.
leadParagraph: Joren is broad as a doorframe, with soot-black forearms, a leather apron, and pride he usually disguises as irritation.
backgroundParagraph: He forges Wren a proper horn-handled knife and supplies the hinges needed when Hollowfen's boarded cottages begin to open again.
perspectiveParagraph: Joren judges people by whether they know what a tool is for. Pretty costs extra, cuts worse, and earns very little patience at his anvil.
pullquote: Pretty costs extra and cuts worse.

missions.title: Joren's Forge
missions.summary: Forge Wren a proper curved foraging knife.

missions.title: Two Boards Come Down
missions.summary: Make hinges for the first repaired cottages.

quotes.text: Pretty costs extra and cuts worse.
Come back tomorrow. And don't tell anyone you commissioned it like charity.
quotes.context: Protecting his pride after accepting the work.

quotes.text: Old nails. Rotten frame. No one respects hinges until they fail.

home: On the road and Lord Aldric's estate
work: Tax collection, ledgers, and official deliveries
tagline: A tired professional carrying exact figures through an inexact hardship.
leadParagraph: Master Voss is gaunt, plain, and controlled, with a drab coat and a ledger satchel held close.
backgroundParagraph: He arrives in Hollowfen as Lord Aldric's collector, counts what the village owes without raising his voice, and later returns carrying a sealed letter.
perspectiveParagraph: Voss separates sympathy from duty because his work requires it. His smallest changes in posture say more than a speech would.
pullquote: I am not unsympathetic, but I am also not paid to be sympathetic.

missions.title: Twelve Silver by Yule
missions.summary: Demand twelve silver and name the Wenmar cottage as security.

missions.title: A Sealed Letter
missions.summary: Deliver Lord Aldric's letter to Wren in person.

quotes.text: I am not unsympathetic, but I am also not paid to be sympathetic.
I was already doing so.
quotes.context: When Wren tells him to wait for payment.

quotes.text: I am not here to collect.
quotes.context: Returning to Hollowfen with Aldric's letter.

work: Trade, market orders, and wagon routes
tagline: The worldly trader who can price a mushroom, a story, and an open road.
leadParagraph: Theo is a well-groomed traveler with a trim beard, a better coat than most Hollowfen villagers, and a ledger never far from hand.
backgroundParagraph: His wagon gives Wren access to buyers beyond the village and turns unusual mushrooms into a trade that can survive the axle ruts.
perspectiveParagraph: Theo prefers an honest bargain because it saves time. He sees opportunity quickly and is careful to name the cost when it matters.

missions.title: The Trader's Ledger
missions.summary: Buy Wren's basket and establish a regular route through Hollowfen.

missions.title: Theo's Capital
missions.summary: Offer Wren a larger market and state its cost plainly.

quotes.text: Cruel answer. I respect it.
quotes.context: When Wren says she found Goldfoot near trees.

quotes.text: When it saves time.
It would cost Hollowfen you.

home: On the road; a guest at the Crooked Pintle
tagline: The quiet traveler following a family path that remembers feet better than names.
leadParagraph: Hollin is pale, dark-haired, and contained, a traveler who arrives with one satchel and no interest in buying or selling.
backgroundParagraph: She has walked through three villages looking for Wren and for a place her grandmother named when she thought no one was listening.
perspectiveParagraph: Hollin listens for what paths, old pages, and careful silences retain. She speaks sparingly and enters remembered places kindly.
pullquote: I think the path remembers feet better than names.

missions.title: A Stranger at the Inn
missions.summary: Arrive at the Crooked Pintle and ask for Wren by name.

missions.title: Hollin's Inheritance
missions.summary: Share the page and name her grandmother left her.

missions.title: The Witch's Cottage
missions.summary: Follow the old path with Wren and Almy.

missions.title: The Wend's True Course
missions.summary: Help trace the river's remembered line.

missions.title: The Source of the Wend
missions.summary: Walk upstream with Wren to the clear-cut.

quotes.text: I am walking. And looking for a place my grandmother used to name when she thought no one was listening.
quotes.context: Explaining why she came to Hollowfen.

quotes.text: I think the path remembers feet better than names.
Not broken. Loosed.
quotes.context: Correcting Wren at the source of the Wend.

tagline: A kind, rigid priest trying not to arrive late to wisdom or early to harm.
leadParagraph: Father Calden is lean, white-haired, and formal, with a dark cassock, wooden cross, and doubt carried plainly in his face.
backgroundParagraph: He knew Wren's father and baptized Wren, but the old wood lore troubles him enough to lock the chapel garden and ask her to stop.
perspectiveParagraph: Calden is wary of knowledge he cannot bless and ashamed of harm done under authority. His caution can close a gate, but it can also make him examine why it was closed.
pullquote: Being late to wisdom. Being early to harm.

missions.title: Father Calden's Doubt
missions.summary: Warn Wren away from the deeper wood and close the chapel garden.

missions.title: The Chapel Garden Opens
missions.summary: Read the old records and return the garden key.

quotes.text: The edge of a thing is not the thing.
quotes.context: Warning Wren about the deeper wood.

quotes.text: Being late to wisdom. Being early to harm.
Because it was useful to people who were not powerful.
quotes.context: Explaining why the old knowledge was buried.

tagline: The memory of Hollowfen made visible in pencil, ink, and careful arithmetic.
leadParagraph: Elder Pell is stooped, narrow, and slow-moving, with a ledger under one arm and a pencil ready for whatever the village becomes next.
backgroundParagraph: He keeps the restoration account at the village well, prices boards and hinges, and records changes only when they have become real enough to deserve ink.
perspectiveParagraph: Pell distrusts hope as a claim but respects it as evidence. One shutter is not a village; two shutters may be a sentence beginning.
pullquote: Two shutters is a sentence beginning.

missions.title: Two Boards Come Down
missions.summary: Keep the restoration ledger and fund the first new shutters.

missions.title: The First Festival in Three Years
missions.summary: Record the season's public turning point.

quotes.text: The Wenmar house wants shutters more than it wants sympathy.
Two shutters is a sentence beginning.
This year's page may need more ink.

tagline: A polished landholder who treats every future as a set of negotiable terms.
leadParagraph: Lord Aldric is educated, well-fed, and composed, dressed in dark wool and velvet with a signet ring always close to the business at hand.
backgroundParagraph: His estate's taxes and upstream interests reach Hollowfen long before he meets Wren, first through Voss's ledger and then through a carefully written offer.
perspectiveParagraph: Aldric believes prosperity can be arranged by ownership, protection, and arithmetic. His danger lies in calm certainty rather than raised voices.
pullquote: A letter is not an acquaintance. Merely an opening bid.

missions.title: Twelve Silver by Yule
missions.summary: Apply estate pressure through Voss's tax demand.

missions.title: The Wend's True Course
missions.summary: Enter the river story as the name behind the upstream works.

missions.title: A Sealed Letter
missions.summary: Send Wren a letter through Master Voss.

missions.title: The Lord's Offer
missions.summary: Set out terms for patronage and control of Hollowfen's trade.

missions.title: The Source of the Wend
missions.summary: Stand behind the estate marks found at the clear-cut.

missions.title: The Meeting
missions.summary: Meet Wren at the manor and negotiate Hollowfen's future.

quotes.text: A letter is not an acquaintance. Merely an opening bid.
I can make Hollowfen prosperous.
quotes.context: Stating his offer in its simplest form.

quotes.text: That is rarely the first word in a negotiation.

tagline: The frail elder whose recovery makes the village's new knowledge painfully human.
leadParagraph: Edda's grandfather is thin, white-haired, and usually seen propped beneath a quilt, with the face and hands of a real person rather than an abstract village need.
backgroundParagraph: When he stops eating, Edda asks Wren for help. Brightspore, careful preparation, and a bowl of soup turn the lesson into the first named life saved by mushroom knowledge.
perspectiveParagraph: His authored presence is quiet and domestic: a pillow stack, a tonic bottle, both hands around a bowl, and the visible difference between illness and recovery.

missions.title: Brightspore at the Bedside
missions.summary: Receive the Brightspore tonic and recover enough to eat a full bowl.

missions.title: Edda Asks
missions.summary: Be well enough for Edda to leave the bedside and begin her apprenticeship.

tagline: One third of a household made vulnerable by a line in an estate ledger.
leadParagraph: The Wenmar father is one of three worn rural villagers seated together when Voss names their cottage as security for Hollowfen's tax.
backgroundParagraph: His presentation belongs to the family's shared story state: guarded at the inn bench, then visible again at the cottage if the household remains secure.
perspectiveParagraph: Keep the performance specific and restrained: clasped hands, guarded posture, and attention fixed on the home represented by one small key.

missions.title: Twelve Silver by Yule
missions.summary: Wait with the family while Wren tries to prevent seizure of the cottage.

missions.title: Two Boards Come Down
missions.summary: Share the Wenmar household's repaired-shutter world state.

tagline: One third of a family waiting to learn whether home has become collateral.
leadParagraph: The Wenmar mother is part of the worn rural family gathered at the inn when the quarterly tax puts their cottage at risk.
backgroundParagraph: Her authored staging is shared with the family: the cold hearth, the guarded bench, and the threshold of the cottage after the tax is resolved.
perspectiveParagraph: Her performance should remain observational rather than melodramatic, using close-held hands and a covered mouth to make the household's stakes legible.

missions.title: Twelve Silver by Yule
missions.summary: Wait with the family while Wren tries to prevent seizure of the cottage.

missions.title: Two Boards Come Down
missions.summary: Share the Wenmar household's repaired-shutter world state.

tagline: The teenager who watches his boots while adults count the price of home.
leadParagraph: The Wenmar son is the teenage member of the family seated nearest the cold hearth during Voss's tax demand.
backgroundParagraph: He belongs to the same two authored states as his parents: the tense inn bench before payment and the occupied cottage after the household is secured.
perspectiveParagraph: His visual direction is deliberately quiet: a guarded posture and eyes lowered to boots he studies as if they, too, might be taken.

missions.title: Twelve Silver by Yule
missions.summary: Wait with the family while Wren tries to prevent seizure of the cottage.

missions.title: Two Boards Come Down
missions.summary: Share the Wenmar household's repaired-shutter world state.

tagline: A working villager shaped by water, lye, and the weight of wet linen.
leadParagraph: An adult laundress in plain rural layers, suited to wash-day activity near Hollowfen's well, lanes, and river.
backgroundParagraph: Use this villager for household labor, water carrying, cottage routines, and practical festival crowd variation.
perspectiveParagraph: The silhouette should remain grounded in work: rolled sleeves, a balanced stance, and simple laundry props.

tagline: A field worker built for long rows, bad soil, and practical village labor.
leadParagraph: An adult field hand in durable rural clothing, intended for cultivated plots, repair crews, and harvest staging.
backgroundParagraph: Use this villager where Hollowfen needs visible agricultural labor rather than another named story character.
perspectiveParagraph: The read is occupational and physical: sun-worn layers, planted feet, and tools carried for use rather than display.

tagline: A textile worker whose place in the village is visible in thread, cloth, and mended layers.
leadParagraph: An adult weaver in simple Hollowfen clothing, suitable for cottage interiors, market activity, and seated handwork.
backgroundParagraph: Use this villager to represent the quiet textile labor behind ordinary clothes, sacks, blankets, and repairs.
perspectiveParagraph: The presentation should emphasize measured hands and material awareness without assigning a private history.

tagline: A mill worker for the village's remembered industry and visible recovery.
leadParagraph: An adult laborer suited to grain sacks, mill-yard work, loading, sweeping, and repair activity around the Tobin mill.
backgroundParagraph: Use this villager when the mill or village economy needs a working body rather than an anonymous crowd silhouette.
perspectiveParagraph: The character should read through sturdy layers, a ready stance, and props tied directly to grain and lifting.

tagline: An orchard worker observed through practical layers, pruning tools, and seasonal labor.
leadParagraph: An adult orchard worker suited to tree rows, baskets, pruning, and village-edge agricultural routines.
backgroundParagraph: Use this villager to broaden Hollowfen's food work beyond fields, kitchens, and mushroom gathering.
perspectiveParagraph: Keep the visual language seasonal and task-led, with no implied biography beyond the work shown on screen.

tagline: A weathered flock tender for Hollowfen's pasture edges and road approaches.
leadParagraph: An adult shepherd in weather-ready rural layers, intended for pasture boundaries, lanes, and returning-at-dusk routines.
backgroundParagraph: Use this villager with flock, staff, bundle, or gate activity wherever the village needs pastoral labor.
perspectiveParagraph: The design should communicate exposure to wind and distance through posture and clothing rather than invented history.

tagline: A young stable hand scaled for quick chores, feed sacks, and animal care.
leadParagraph: A teenage stable hand in worn work clothes, designed for smaller stature and practical animal-care activity.
backgroundParagraph: Use this villager for feed, brushing, mucking, gate, and wagon-adjacent routines rather than adult labor staging.
perspectiveParagraph: The character should read as young through scale and motion while remaining a worker, not a sentimental child figure.

tagline: A young gooseherd for the village lanes, commons, and lively crowd edges.
leadParagraph: A teenage gooseherd in plain rural dress, scaled below the adult cast for flock-following and lane activity.
backgroundParagraph: Use this villager with geese, baskets, small deliveries, and festival errands that need an age-appropriate silhouette.
perspectiveParagraph: The identity remains observational: alert posture, practical footwear, and attention directed toward animals and paths.

tagline: A basket maker whose craft sits quietly beneath Hollowfen's foraging economy.
leadParagraph: An adult basket maker intended for seated wicker work, market display, cottage thresholds, and repair activity.
backgroundParagraph: Use this villager to make the material culture behind Wren's baskets and village carrying work visible.
perspectiveParagraph: The silhouette should favor capable hands, bundled reeds, and compact tools without adding a personal legend.

tagline: An older carpenter for shutters, frames, benches, and the village's visible repairs.
leadParagraph: An older adult carpenter in hard-worn work clothing, suited to restoration sites and measured bench work.
backgroundParagraph: Use this villager beside boards, shutters, frames, and repair props as Hollowfen's occupied buildings return.
perspectiveParagraph: The presentation should emphasize practiced economy of movement and tools arranged for work, not ornament.

tagline: A hearth worker connecting clay vessels, bread ovens, and the ordinary shape of village meals.
leadParagraph: An older adult potter-baker suited to kiln, oven, market, and inn staging with bowls, crocks, and loaves.
backgroundParagraph: Use this villager wherever food service and household wares need a grounded craft presence in the crowd.
perspectiveParagraph: The visual read comes from strong hands, flour or clay-ready layers, and stable working posture.

tagline: A river worker defined by rope, wet banks, and the practical edge of the Wend.
leadParagraph: An adult river worker in weathered layers, intended for bankside hauling, rope work, crossings, and flood cleanup.
backgroundParagraph: Use this villager around the Wend and village approaches when water-related labor needs a human presence.
perspectiveParagraph: Keep the character task-focused through rope, pole, bundle, and footing rather than a narrated past.

tagline: A young craft worker for the shoes, straps, and small repairs that keep a village moving.
leadParagraph: A young adult cobbler's apprentice in plain work clothes, suited to seated leatherwork, market errands, and shop thresholds.
backgroundParagraph: Use this villager to add a junior craft silhouette without assigning story dialogue or a private arc.
perspectiveParagraph: The observational read is close handwork, small tools, and attention fixed on worn leather rather than the crowd.

tagline: A mixed-work villager for hives, small animals, and the edges between garden and pasture.
leadParagraph: An adult beekeeper-herder in practical rural dress, intended for garden edges, pasture gates, and honey-market staging.
backgroundParagraph: Use this villager where Hollowfen's small-scale animal and hive work should be visible without creating a named role.
perspectiveParagraph: The design should stay calm and work-led, using protective layers, a skep, or herding props as the identifying language.

tagline: A soot-marked fuel worker for wood piles, kiln mounds, and the darker edge of village labor.
leadParagraph: An adult charcoal burner in smoke-worn layers, intended for wood-edge fuel work and deliveries to forge, kitchen, and hearth.
backgroundParagraph: Use this villager beside sacks, timber, kiln mounds, and fuel carts where Hollowfen's heat supply becomes visible.
perspectiveParagraph: The identity is conveyed through soot, heavy cloth, and long-handled tools, with no invented personal story.
